error.signature: AI confuses China's 240-hour transit-without-visa policy with general visa-free entry, or misses the specific eligible airports and onward-travel requirements
error.regex: (china.*transit|240 hour twov|china.*visa free|china.*72 hour|china.*144 hour|twov china|china.*visa policy 2024|chinese visa exemption)

verdict.summary: China's 240-hour Transit Without Visa (TWOV, 过境免签, expanded December 2024) lets nationals of 54 countries transit through 60+ Chinese ports for up to 10 days, provided they hold an onward ticket to a third country (NOT the country of origin). Separately, full visa-free entry of up to 30 days now applies to several countries (FR, DE, IT, NL, ES, MY, SG, NZ, AU, JP, KR — list expanded several times in 2024-2025). Verify each nationality + entry mode separately.

dead_ends.action: Tell a US passport holder they qualify for China's full 30-day visa-free entry
dead_ends.why_fails: As of 2025, the 30-day mutual visa-free arrangement applies to specific countries (FR, DE, IT, NL, ES, MY, SG, NZ, AU, JP, KR, ...) — list updated frequently by NIA. US is NOT on the visa-free list (still requires L visa or 240-hour TWOV transit). Confirm at NIA en.nia.gov.cn before each trip.

dead_ends.action: Use 240-hour TWOV planning to fly into Beijing then back to your origin country
dead_ends.why_fails: TWOV requires onward travel to a THIRD country (not the country of origin). A round-trip US ↔ China violates TWOV; immigration refuses entry. Workaround: third-country leg (e.g. US → China → Korea → US) qualifies.

dead_ends.action: Apply 240-hour TWOV at any Chinese airport without checking eligible ports
dead_ends.why_fails: TWOV is available at 60+ designated ports (Beijing PEK/PKX, Shanghai PVG/SHA, Guangzhou CAN, Shenzhen SZX, Chengdu CTU, Xian XIY, plus many others). Some smaller airports don't offer TWOV. Confirm port-by-port via NIA.

workarounds.action: Check the current visa-free list at en.nia.gov.cn before each trip — list expanded multiple times in 2024 and may continue to. Verify your nationality + intended duration + ports of entry.

240-hour TWOV: prepare onward ticket to a third country (not origin), accommodation reservation, passport with 6+ months validity. Apply at the immigration desk on arrival; airline staff verify before boarding to China.

If neither visa-free nor TWOV applies, apply for a regular L (tourist), M (business), or X (student) visa at the Chinese consulate. 10-year multi-entry L visas are common for US nationals. Process: 4-10 business days.
